observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and the MA agreed to:

Prepare Schedules with complete details for Guaranty Deposit/ Security Payable; and 

Exert extra effort to trace the outstanding balances to whom the management was indebted to, and provide the details that would warrant its validity.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:

The MDC and the MEO to ensure that priority PPAs to be included in the AIP are procurement-and-implementation-ready to avoid implementation issues such as site problems;  

The Implementing Office shall regularly review the AIP for 20 per cent DF and monitor the implementation of the PPAs to ensure timely request for procurement process; and 

Fast track the implementation of those PPAs which have not yet been started and have remained unfinished as at year-end.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the MGSO to notify the Audit Team of the deliveries of goods and services within 24 hours after its acceptance thru submission of AIR and its supporting documents such as PO or contracts, delivery receipts/sales invoice/charge invoice, and purchase requests, for proper evaluation thereof.

Management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to finish the RPTAR and Certified list of RPT Delinquencies, and furnish a copy to the MA as basis to draw a JEV to record the correct balance of RPT Receivable and SET Receivable.

We recommended Management to:

Conduct re-orientation seminar focusing on the Appendix C of the manual which provides for the Matrix of Reports and Forms showing the record/report title, who shall submit or accomplish the report, number of copies to be distributed and to whom it will be distributed, the frequency and due date of submission and the remarks or purpose of the record/report to know the extent of their duties and responsibilities;

Provide adequate personnel complement in the Accounting Unit to ensure timely, complete, and accurate preparation and submission of Barangay Financial Statements to the office of the auditor; and

Require explanation/ justification from concerned Barangays who still failed to comply despite constant monitoring and supervision.

Management thru the LCE to direct the municipality’s GFPS to conduct gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance.

Management requires the BAC to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within the schedule set in accordance with the said regulation.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed that the LCE ensure the compliance of the LGU with the policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset Management System by necessitating:
The ME to provide the MA and the General Services Office (GSO) with complete details as to description, cost segregation of road components for road projects, and other information necessary for the proper accounting and recording of all RNs of the LGU.
The MA to:
Obtain additional details on the lumpsum amount of P2.558 million and update accounting records;
Convene with the LCE, the ME, and other concerned offices regarding the P8.991 million RNs that have already been turned-over to barangays to facilitate adjustment in the accounting books;
Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and ME, on the details of the P0.921 million road lots booked up under Land account to identify which RN to record the component with, and prepare the necessary journal entries to adjust the same in the accounting books; and
Provide the disclosure paragraph on RNs in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular 2015-008 and submit to COA.
The MGSO to:
Coordinate with the MA on the RN projects that has been turned-over to barangay LGUs to facilitate exclusion from the property records; and
Regularly update the RLRN.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The MHMRO to:
Prepare a Master List of earned leave credits of the officials and employees of the LGU as of December 31, 2024, and submit to the Local Finance Committee (LFC) thru the MBO for consideration in the appropriation for the succeeding years until the earned leave credits are backed up by appropriation and funds;
Prepare the quarterly leave credits earned by officials and employees, with peso equivalent and supported with a copy of the respective leave cards, as of every end of the quarter, starting CY 2025; and
Submit the Master list and the quarter report of earned leave credits of officials and employees to the MA for recording in the books subject to the availability of appropriation and funds certified by the MBO and Municipal Treasurer (MT).
The MA, upon receipt of the Master list and the quarter report of earned leave credits of officials and employees from the MHRMO to:
Record in the books the liability duly supported by a certification from the MBO and MT on the availability of appropriation and funds; and
In case the appropriation and funds are insufficient, continuously monitor the recognition of earned leave until such time that the all earned leave credits are recorded and funded.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Management agreed that:
The MGSO to establish a filing and monitoring mechanism for all deliveries, and provide the details to the MA to facilitate the set-up of AP.
The MBO to allocate funds for the balance to facilitate recording of AP.
The MA to:
Revisit, validate and confirm the existence of long outstanding payable accounts in the books and if found non-existent, consider processing for reversion to the unappropriated surplus of GF;
Prepare journal entries to recognize the liability of P0.969 million and the corresponding asset and adjusting journal entries for prior period, subject to availability funds;
Examine and provide additional details on the booked-up AP amounting to P106,703.00; and
Ensure the attachment of JEVs with the corresponding supporting documents prior to submission to COA.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the MA agreed to set-up Allowance for Impairment – Other Receivables for fair presentation of the Other Receivables account in the FS.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Management agreed that:
The MT to:
Fast-track the preparation of schedule for delinquent RP taxpayers;
Furnish the MA with the schedule of delinquent RPT for the preparation of subsidiary accounting records; and
Prepare a summary of all the RPTAR and Taxpayer’s Index Cards at the beginning of each year, and furnish the MA for the set-up of current year receivables.
The MA to:
Prepare and maintain the schedules to support the report balances of the RPT and SET Receivables upon receipt from the MTO; and
Moving forward, prepare and submit to COA the journal entry vouchers on the current year set-up of RPT and SET Receivables, supported with the summary of RPTAR from the MTO.

Recommendations
We recommended and management agreed to disclose in the Notes to FS the following:
Reconciliation of the SCBAA and SFPer as per paragraph 14c and 47 of IPSAS 24; and
Reconciliation of the net cash flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit per paragraph 29 of IPSAS 2.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The MA to:
Record the issuances of inventories only when supported by SSMI with RIS and/or distribution list; and
Prepare relevant JEVs and submit the same with supporting documents to COA.
The MGSO to:
Coordinate with the MA for the list of delivered goods still recognized as inventory and determine whether the same are still in stock. If otherwise, prepare SSMI and submit to MA;
Instruct all end-users thru issuance of a memorandum to clarify procedures required in monitoring the issuances of goods; and
Establish an effective mechanism to ensure proper and timely reporting of supply and inventory issuances on all deliveries by complying with the general process to be followed in the control of inventory provided in Section 122 of the M NGAS for LGUs.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the MA agreed to:
Effect the necessary adjustments on the cited causes to arrive at an adjusted and reconciled balance of the two reciprocal accounts; and
Establish clear-cut internal policies in the periodic reconciliation of accounts to eliminate the above noted differences in the accounts and prevent the same from recurrence.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Management agreed to ensure that:
The MA investigate the noted reconciling items by:
Establishing completeness of recording the disbursements by comparing file copies of the bank-validated ADA during the months for which the difference in reconciling items for disbursements occurred with the recorded expenses in the agency books;
Establishing completeness of recording receipts by comparing prior submissions of Reports of Collections and Deposits during the months for which the difference in reconciling items occurred with the recorded deposits in the agency books;
Performing the necessary procedures to ensure prompt action on the reconciling items identified on the BRS;
Upon identification, submit to COA the journal entry vouchers prepared for the reconciling items noted in the BRS, with supporting documents; and
Moving forward, ensuring that supporting documents for identified reconciling items are verified and valid prior to making adjusting journal entries.
The MT to:
Provide the MA with information and corresponding source documents on the fund transfers; and
Moving forward, ensure that supporting documents for identified reconciling items are verified and valid prior to making adjusting entries in the cashbook.
Both the MA and MTO to establish an agreement on the monthly reconciliation of records to prevent reoccurrence of accumulated unreconciled items.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to continue with the implementation of COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts, and ensure that:
The MA to:
Prepare and maintain the PPELCs for all Land and Infrastructure assets of the LGU; and
Obtain the RSPI and submit as supporting documents to recognized expense for semi-expendable properties.
The MGSO to:
Complete the RPCPPE to facilitate reconciliation of the PPEs of the LGU;
Prepare, maintain, and update the Property Cards for all classes of PPE; and
Prepare the RegSPI, SPC, RPCSP, and keep file copies of ICS for all semi-expendable property, especially those already expensed from the accounting records and prepare RSPI for all issuances of semi-expendable property which shall be the basis in preparing the JEV to recognize the issuances.
We also recommended, and the MA and MGSO agreed to reconcile the inventory count per RPCPPE with the accounting and property records, in accordance with Item 6.3 of COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct he MA to:

Make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the entries made; and

Properly review the transactions before posting in the books of account in accordance with IPSAS No. 1, COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and PD 1445.

We recommended, and the MA agreed to draw a JEV to record and adjust the reconciling item in the agency books and ensure that reconciling items are timely adjusted to generate accurate financial reporting.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require:

The MT/MGSO to conduct an inventory taking and prepare the RPCI – Accountable Forms; 

The MA to reconcile the supplies ledger cards against the RPCI, and CRAAF and provide adjusting entries thereafter; and 

The MT revise the submitted CRAAF and ensure that all the accountable forms of the LGU are reported therein; and submit the CRAAF together with the RAAF of the collectors not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the GFPS to formulate a GAD Agenda for the remaining term of the LCE in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and be made as basis in the preparation of the annual GPB.

We recommended, and the management agreed to:

Endorse to the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the SB to endorse the passing of an ordinance localizing RA 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places; and

Include in the ordinance provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the BAC to:

Follow the proper conduct for the procurement of goods thru Negotiated Procurement in accordance with the procedures in Section 53, of RIRR, RA 9184;

Post the Invitation to Bid at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Procuring Entity concerned for seven (7) calendar days as certified by the head of the BAC Secretariat of the Procuring Entity concerned per Section 21.1(b) of the RIRR, RA 9184; and

Strictly require the BAC-TWG to conduct and exercise utmost prudence in the post-qualification of the S/LCB in order to properly determine whether the S/LCB complies with all the eligibility requirements and conditions specified in the RIRR, of RA 9184.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:

Require the suppliers/ contractors to post warranty security in accordance with Section 62 of the RIRR of RA 9184, which shall remain effective for minimum period of one (1) year from the date of acceptance by the Procuring Entity of the delivered supplies;

Ensure that all goods procured are covered with warranty securities in compliance with the rules on warranties and all other related requirements provided in the RIRR of RA 9184; and

Comply with the rules on warranties and all other related requirements provided in the IRR of RA 9184.
We recommended, and Management agreed to require the supplier to make repairs on the defective and non-operable fog light, left side rear signal lights and 4-wheel drive mechanism.

Management thru the LCE to Create and establish a Monitoring and Evaluation Team in compliance with the provisions of Section 4.1.A.2. and Section 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to implement the GAD PPAs efficiently and effectively.

Management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to finish the Real Property Tax Account Register and Certified list of Real Property Tax Delinquencies, and furnish a copy to the Municipal Accountant as basis to draw a JEV to record the correct balance of Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable.

The LCE to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the accountable officers to prepare and submit monthly RAAF and CRAAF for all accountable forms in accordance with Section 50 and 51 of Volume II of the New Government Accounting System Manual.

Management thru the LCE to direct the municipality’s GFPS to conduct gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance.

Management thru the LCE to:

1 	Reconstitute the composition of the existing GFPS ExCom and GFPS TWG; and

2 	Create and establish a Monitoring and Evaluation Team in compliance with the provisions of Section 4.1.A.2. and Section 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to implement the GAD PPAs efficiently and effectively.

Management directs the Municipal Accountant to:

1	Exert efforts to retrieve the complete details on the above-mentioned assets necessary for the computation of depreciation expense; and 

2	Recognize depreciation expense and corresponding accumulated depreciation for the proper valuation of all depreciable PPE in the financial statement.

Management requiring the Accounting Unit to exert extra effort to trace the unidentified/ undeterminable balances, verify the supporting documents to establish validity, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries, thereof, to have a fair presentation of accounts, and so as not to defeat the purpose of the Financial Statement in providing vital information to its users.

Management to direct the Municipal Accountant:

1	To review the validity of the accounts payable and if found to have no valid claims against the LGU, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus. 

2	For those with valid claims, communicate with the creditors to confirm its existence and make appropriate actions.
Management directs the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions stated in IPSAS 1 paragraphs 70 and 80.

Management directs the Municipal Accountant to:

1	Prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the reclassification of the completed projects from CIP accounts to appropriate PPE accounts; and

2	Recognize and record the corresponding depreciation and accumulated depreciation for the completed projects.

Moving forward, we recommended that the management:

1	Direct the Municipal Health Officer coordinate with the Provincial Health Office to ask for the JEV of the donated vaccines and supplies and the same be communicated to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts of the Municipal Government;

2	Moreover, if securing of the required documents is not feasible, management to conduct appraisal of the vaccines and supplies to determine the amount to be booked-up and facilitate immediate recording of the same; and

3	In both circumstances, the transactions are to be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

Management to:

1	Unload the Liquidating Officer and Collecting Officer of either collecting or disbursing function, whichever is appropriate;

2	Not grant any cash advance to a designated Liquidating Officer and Collecting Officer to avoid incompatible functions; and

3	Ensure that there are no existing cases of incompatible functions particularly for collections and disbursements.

Management:

1	Direct the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and all Department Heads to ensure that the list of ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year is prepared with complete details and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year and quarterly period thereafter; and

2 	Inform the Office of the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the infrastructure project's award or before starting the program/ activity that the appropriate project signboards and public notices are already posted.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the required subsidiary ledgers and schedules to support its accuracy and propriety of account balances as reflected in the financial statements.

Management:

a. Direct the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure that the list of on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year is prepared with complete details and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year;

b. Inform the Office of the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the infrastructure project's award or before starting the program/ activity that the appropriate project signboards and public notices are already posted; and

c. The Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit of the monthly monitoring report on PPAs with complete information.

Management to:  

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit timely the monthly bank reconciliation statements for all depository accounts on various funds of the Municipality including copies of the bank statements, debit/credit memos, cancelled and paid checks and all other supporting documents; 
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements of the two (2) bank accounts including copies of the bank statements, debit/credit memos, cancelled and paid checks and all other supporting documents; and

Have proper coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant regarding the request for depository banks to furnish copies of BS immediately after the end of each month for the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements.

The Local Chief Executive:

a. Require the concerned department heads to submit the status report on their audit findings and recommendations to facilitate the preparation, consolidation, and submission of the AAPSI, in compliance with section 89 of the General Provision of RA No. 11465, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of 2020; and

b. Moreover, strictly monitor compliance on each audit observation on a regular basis to facilitate accomplishment on the status of implementation of the said audit observations.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within the schedule set in accordance with the said regulation.

Management directs the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that LCE through the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board adopt the following courses of action:

Make follow-up/representation with the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) to expedite the approval of its SWM Plan; and

Fast track the implementations of the SWM facilities/structures as envisioned and budgeted in the SWMP.

3.8	We recommended that the:
Local Chief Executive
Immediately create an Inventory Committee with adequate number of members and representatives from the Accounting and Property Division/Unit; and
Consider to temporarily relieve the members of the Inventory Committee of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed.
The Inventory Committee:
Conduct a complete physical count of PPE based on the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
Prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and reconcile the same with the Property Division/Unit and Accounting Unit records.
Property Unit/GSO
Prepare and maintain Property Cards for all items of PPE;
Prepare and submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) using the prescribed format for all PPE found unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed; and
Work together with the Office of the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the PCs with the latter’s PPELCs.
Accounting Unit/Office of the Municipal Accountant
Provide complete details in the supporting schedules of PPE accounts and submit together with the financial statements;
Prepare and update PPELCs for all classes of PPE;
Take up necessary adjusting entries based on the results of the physical count; and
Work together with the GSO to reconcile the PPELCs with the latter’s PCs.

6.7	We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the required subsidiary ledgers and schedules to support its accuracy and propriety of account balances as reflected in the financial statements.

7.6	We recommended that management thru the LCE to:
a) 	Reconstitute the composition of the existing GFPS ExCom and GFPS TWG; and
b) 	Create and establish a Monitoring and Evaluation Team in compliance with the provisions of Section 4.1.A.2. and Section 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to implement the GAD PPAs efficiently and effectively.

8.10	We recommended that management thru the LCE to:
Direct the municipality’s GFPS to conduct gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance;
Equip the GFPS with the in-depth knowledge and capacity on GAD, they need to undergo trainings and seminars such as those relating to gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and the use of various GAD tools for the LGU’s GAD implementation of PAPs; and
Revisit and strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9710 and other related provisions of law.

9.15	We recommended that management to:
a. 	Coordinate with the BFP, re-conduct evaluation and inspection on the municipal buildings’ fire safety standards and implement fire preventive measures like the creation of permanent stairways and/or improvised ladder to avoid the risk of losing lives and properties in cases of man-made and/or natural fire calamities; and
b. 	Provide the necessary and applicable firefighting equipment/materials installed on conspicuous places, and post as well an emergency evacuation plan where anyone can likewise easily access.

11.13	We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that the management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and certified correct the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Fund;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to determine and transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and MOOE of the LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund at year-end; and
Allocate budget for the establishment of a permanent evacuation center intended for disaster preparedness and response in accordance with the set-forth standards provided in DILG Memorandum Circular 122, series of 2018; and
Prioritize the hiring and filling-up of the newly created item for LDRRMO.

12.8	We revised our prior years’ audit recommendations that Management:
Prioritize the close coordination among offices to carefully plan and review PPAs to be included in the AIP, set specific and realistic timeline for every program/project based on approved POW;
Consider creating or designating monitoring committee to monitor the implementation of those identified PPAs of the 20% Development Fund from CY 2021, 2020, 2019, 2018, 2017 and prior years and reprogrammed projects and submit monitoring report so that its causes of delays are adequately addressed; and
Reprogrammed those appropriations that cannot be attained or implemented.

13.6	We reiterate our prior years’ audit recommendations that the Management:
a. 	Direct the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the list of ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year is prepared with complete details and submitted to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year and quarterly period thereafter; and
b. 	Inform the Office of the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the infrastructure project's award or before starting the program/ activity that the appropriate project signboards and public notices are already posted.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit timely the monthly bank reconciliation statements for all depository accounts on various funds of the Municipality including copies of the bank statements, debit/credit memos, cancelled and paid checks and all other supporting documents;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statement of one (1) bank account including copies of the bank statements, debit/credit memos, cancelled and paid checks and all other supporting documents; and
Have proper coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant regarding the request for depository banks to furnish copies of BS immediately after the end of each month for the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements.

15.5	We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
a. 	Require the concerned department heads to submit the status report on their audit findings and recommendations to facilitate the preparation, consolidation, and submission of the AAPSI, in compliance with section 91 of the General Provision of RA No. 11518, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of 2021; and
b. 	Moreover, strictly monitor compliance on each audit observation on a regular basis to facilitate accomplishment on the status of implementation of the said audit observations.

17.6	We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that Management direct the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation.

Make follow-up/representation with the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) to expedite the approval of its SWM Plan; and
Fast track the implementations of the SWM facilities/structures as envisioned and budgeted in the SWMP.

Management requires the Accounting Unit to:

a. Exert extra effort to trace the unidentified/undeterminable balances, verify the supporting documents to establish validity, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries, thereof, to have a fair presentation of accounts, and so as not to defeat the purpose of the FS in providing vital information to its users; and

b. Observe proper recording of transactions with complete supporting documents, and maintain respective records that in turn will support the existence and correct valuation of accounts in the FS.

Management directs the Municipal Accountant by:

Recognizing depreciation expense to all depreciable PPE accounts to ensure fairness of presentation in the financial statements; and

Instituting/improving control measures on asset recording for readily available data as reference in recognizing periodic depreciation.

Management to:

a. Submit all the liquidation documents on cash advance granted under LDRRMF within the prescribed period; 

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the correctness and completeness of the supporting liquidation documents before recording in the books of the Municipality; and

c. Direct the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the cash advance liquidation with complete documentation.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide the required disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements as provided for in the fore-mentioned regulations.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that management coordinate with GAD Focal Point:

a) In assigning a responsible person per office in gathering all the relevant information to be used in establishing GAD database; and

b) Designating a responsible person to collect and consolidate all the relevant information from the different offices to be used in establishing GAD database.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Maintain complete SL for each special account in the General Fund; 

Prepare the corresponding financial statements to form part of the schedules of the General Fund; and 

Enforce the provision of Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445 on the withholding of salaries of all Municipal Officials deemed responsible for non-reporting of agency accounts.

We recommended that Management:

a. To submit justification on the delayed completion of the above-mentioned projects and henceforth provide effective feedback mechanism for prompt resolution of issues that may arise during project implementation stage;

b. Ensure that the cause of delays is addressed to effect remedial action for immediate completion of all ADM on-going projects; and

c. Conduct inventory of work accomplished by the contractor and impose liquidated damages equal to at least one tenth (1/10) of one percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of works for every day of delay pursuant to Item 8.1 of Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 if warranted.

Management require the following:

a.	The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile entries in the cashbooks and general ledgers at least on a quarterly basis; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the management that the Municipal Accountant shall withhold the salaries and other emoluments due to the concerned accountable officers until the same have been fully settled/liquidated. For national government agency employees, the Municipal Treasurer should furnish their Regional Office with the demand letters.
File the request for authority to COA to write-off of dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers of National Government Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporation (GOCCs) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 duly supported with schedule of dormant accounts certified by the Accountant and relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions, as applicable.
As for those who already retired/resigned from the service, make a final demand for its settlement to be sent to their present addresses or new official stations.

We recommend management to require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of its road networks in order to ascertain the existence of the balance of the account Road Networks.
Moreover, direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components. Likewise, require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Services Officer at the end of accounting period.

We recommend that management shall execute a contract of lease between the municipality and the market stall occupants in order to effect the terms and conditions especially the payment of rent as stipulated there in that shall contribute to its collection effectiveness.

We recommend that the LDRRMO shall take all necessary steps that facilitate local emergency responses through the implementation of P/P/As to reduce the level of risk or effects of disasters and geared towards the prevention of loss and reduction of loss of property brought by natural and human induced disaster.
Further, we recommend that the LDRRMO shall coordinate with barangay officials, schools and private institutions in the formulation of disaster plan as to climate change adaptation in order to implement a well- comprehensive DRRMP that is holistic, integrated and proactive environmental impacts of disaster.

We recommend that the Local Development Council and the Municipal Engineer should closely monitor and pursue the timely and full implementation of all the projects funded under BUB and LGSF and to submit regular status reports to the Municipal Mayor for his information so that appropriate measures can be instituted in the event projects cannot be fully implemented as planned.

We recommend that management, members of the Solid Waste Management Board (SWMB) shall exercise their duties and responsibilities as what has been contemplated in the 10 year SWM Plan to ensure the effective implementation of SWM Programs.
The SWM Board shall also oversee the execution of SWM operations so as to determine whether practices required under R.A. 9003 which are not being followed and lapses in SWM operation as well can be immediately noted and corrected.

We recommend that management shall conduct the gender analysis and sex-disaggregated data for the municipality to ensure that in the preparation stage of the GAD plan for programs, projects and activities shall truly address a well-defined gender issues towards the achievement of the Philippine Plan for Gender-Responsive Development.

We recommend that:
The management should closely monitor and pursue the timely and full implementation of all the programs and activities embodied in the Annual Investment Plan and to submit regular status reports to the Municipal Mayor for his information so that appropriate measures can be instituted to obtain optimal utilization of fund.
Funds are allocated on the basis of approved programs and projects. Thus, when specific PPAs are identified, it is expected that the need for the project must be met within the time duration for its implementation.

We recommend that basic services/priority expenditures enumerated in Section 272 of LGC shall all be considered in the implementation of the fund so as not to deprive the beneficiaries of its benefits.

We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Accountant shall exert more effort in retrieving records to validate the reconciling items as reflected in the bank reconciliation statements. Likewise, observed reconciliation of records between the Accounting Office and the Office of the Municipal Treasurer because this shall ensure the accuracy of recorded data in both records as well as facilitate in the process of validating book reconciling items.

Require all concerned officials and employees to liquidate and/or settle their cash advances immediately. Details are shown in the attached Annex “C”. Also, send follow-up and/or demand letters requiring the personnel concerned to liquidate, otherwise, impose the withholding of salaries and other emoluments due to the cash advances. For those who are no longer connected with the Municipality, demand letters are to be sent again to their present addresses or stations.

We recommend that management should implement measures to ensure prompt reporting, remittances and deposit of collections in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss, theft and embezzlement.

The Municipality should consider designation of enough regular revenue collectors, considering the substantial volume of work that has been currently performed only by a few collectors.

The accountable officers/collectors shall remit/deposit their collections intact on a daily basis, otherwise, unjustified failure to effect the remittance and deposit of collections will subject the accountable officers/collectors concerned for possible administrative and criminal sanctions.

The Municipal Treasurer shall strictly supervise the manner of remittances and deposits by faithfully complying the policies and procedures as afore-cited above to avoid incurring any administrative or criminal liabilities.

We recommend that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer/GSO conduct physical inventory and verify the recorded balances for reconciliation as presented in the Statement of Financial Position to present the correct amount. 

Update all supporting documents and records as mentioned in the above provision cited in order to have a reliable and correct financial condition and operation of the municipality.

The members of the BAC has to follow the conditions sets forth in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 as to the implementation of alternative procurement in order to ensure transparency and competitiveness even with the use of such methods.

Consolidation of all the PPMPs submitted by the various end-user offices must be pursued with the use of APP as one of the procurement strategy in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects to be able the BAC to determine the extent of quantities, quality and cost.

Establish procurement timeliness in consonance with project schedules, including a cut-off date for processing requisitions and issuance of purchase orders to ensure receipt of most procured items in times for the undertaking of projects, programs and activities, with provisions for exceptions only in case of emergencies.

We recommend that the management should look for major funding sources to finance the uncompleted project in order for the project not to remain idle/unutilized. 

Also, evaluate the project to come out with a preventive measures to protect it from the risk of deterioration due to its exposure to fortuitous event.

We recommend that basic services/priority expenditures enumerated in Section 272 of the LGC should be considered in the implementation of the fund so as not to deprive the constituents of its benefit.

Proper coordination must be established between the Bids and Awards Committee, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office regarding the preparation of the report on the P/P/As implemented ad to be implemented by the Municipality.

See to it that reported P/P/As are reconciled against the records maintained by the BAC, Municipal Engineering, Municipal Budget Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Office before submission to COA for audit/validation.

Require the concerned official/Municipal Engineering Office to submit at the beginning of the year in accordance with the provisions of 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and

Provide our Office of any corrections/revisions or changes to the projects to be implemented for our validation/monitoring.

The members of the GAD committee has to faithfully comply the timely and accurate submission of the GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) of the Provincial Government and DILG Regional Offices for its review and approval.

It is also suggested that the committee may invite the municipality assigned DILG or make representation with the DILG to be guided on the preparation of GAD Plans and Budgets (GPBs) since the latter has the authority to review and approve the LGUs GPBS.

Without the approved GAD Plan, the agency may not be guided in the implementation of the activities on whether these are responsive to the gender issues that need to be addressed by the program.

The LDRRMO must take all the necessary steps that facilitate local emergency responses through the implementation of P/P/As to reduce the level of risk or effects of disasters and geared towards the prevention of loss of life and reduction of loss of property brought by natural and human-induced disaster.

Coordinate with barangay officials, schools and private institutions in the formulation of disaster plan as to climate change adaptation in order to implement a well-comprehensive DRRMP that is holistic, integrated and proactive environmental impacts of disasters.

The SWM Board shall also oversee the execution of SWM operations so as to determine whether practices required under R.A. 9003 which are not being followed and lapses in SWM operation as well can be immediately noted and corrected.

We recommend that the members of the Inventory Committee will immediately undertake physical count to obtain the enumerated objectives on physical count. Also, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal treasurer must ensure to establish/set up ledger and property cards as a basis for reconciliation that eventually result to accuracy of recorded data in both records.

Subsequently, the balance reflected in the agency’s financial statements of account Property, Plant and Equipment will be fairly presented and reliable.

Require the concerned officials and employees to settle immediately all their cash advances. In case of failure, imposition of the withholding of their respective salaries and other emoluments due to them be effected until full liquidation/settlement of unliquidated cash advances are made, in consonance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters for the immediate liquidation/settlement of the outstanding cash advances from the concerned officials and employees who are connected with other government agencies and those who are no longer in service.

We recommend that the custodian institute controls over the accountable forms and:

Conduct frequent physical inventory count of unused balances of accountable forms in the hands of the revenue collectors, reconciled to his remittance reports.
Prepare and submit RAAF and CRAAF regularly.
Monitor the movement of all accountable forms.
Reconcile inventory of accountable forms with the accounting records.

We recommend to the management:

To require the GAD focal point to submit yearly the annual GAD Plan and budget of the Municipality to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) of the Provincial Government and DILG Regional Offices for its review and approval.

To require the GAD focal point to submit GAD Accomplishment Reports to the DILG Regional Office for review and consolidation in order to determine and evaluate if such are align to women’s empowerment and gender equality.

We recommend that management should implement measures to ensure prompt reporting, remittances and deposit of collections in order to safeguard such collections from the risk of loss, theft and embezzlement.

Collecting officer/s should be required to prepare RCD’s at the close of each business day and effect remittance of their collections daily.

The Municipality should consider the designation of enough regular revenue collectors, considering the substantial volume of work performed by one (1) collector.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor will give priority of establishing appropriate Archival System in their municipality in order to ensure the safety and protection of all government properties, public documents or records, particularly on the disposal of valueless records, with prior written authority from the Executive Director of the National Archives.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor will direct the Head of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to ensure that only expenditures that have direct bearing or falling within the concept of disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation activities are included in the LDRRMF plan and charged to the Fund.

	We further recommend that every transaction charged to 5% LDRRMF – Fuel and Lubricants be properly supported with details as to what particular activities, proper date and possible fuel and lubricants consumption report in order to conform to the prescribed usage of the fund adhering to establish rules, regulations and procedural guidelines.

We recommend that the prepared Solid Waste Management Plan of the municipality complements and be integrated with that of the developed provincial solid waste management plan, to be submitted to the National Commission for approval.

Moreover, exercise their roles effectively in the capacity to perform being the agent of change particularly on the attitude of program implementation.

Also, exert more efforts to ensure sustainability in their implementation of Waste Management Program.

We recommend that the management refrain from changing expenditures or items that are not covered in the Circular in order that the optional use of the fund will be utilized in priority projects, programs and activities that contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development of the constituents.

We recommend to the management:

To make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balance of affected accounts and strengthen the review and supervision over the work of subordinate personnel to avoid errors in recording transactions.

 That for the meantime the unreconciled amounts are still not adjusted, proper disclosure be made in the Notes to Financial Statement.

Direct the Municipal General Services Office/ Municipal Treasurer to procure commonly use goods in bulk provided it is not beyond the three (3) months consumption and to mitigate shortages upon request by end-user and to take advantage of lower prices in bulk procurement. Small expenses that cannot be paid conveniently by check maybe charged to petty cash fund which the concerned office is currently establishing provided each payment shall not go beyond One Thousand Pesos (P 1,000.00) per petty cash voucher as required by the New Government Accounting System (NGAS).

Likewise, this will ensure the participation of many suppliers every time the agency posts in three (3) conspicuous places its Invitation to BID or Price Quotation
.

We recommend that the management should look for major funding sources to finance the uncompleted project in order for the project not to remain idle/unutilized. 
	
Also, evaluate the project to come out with a preventive measures to protect it from the risk of deterioration due to its exposure to fortuitous event.
